title: Tromsø Arctic Winter Adventure.

trains.name: Trains & Taxi Transfers
trains.blurb: Private airport transfers bookend a week of included Arctic excursions from a single Tromsø base.

transport.1: Private transfer from Tromsø Airport to your hotel.
transport.8: Private transfer from your hotel to Tromsø Airport.

inclusions: Private transfer from Tromsø Airport to hotel on arrival
Tromsø Island Tour with Arctic Cathedral
Long Reindeer Sledding, Reindeer Feeding and Sami Culture
Arctic Fjord Tour with light lunch, 5 hours
Private transfer from hotel to Tromsø Airport on departure
7 nights accommodation in Tromsø with breakfast

days.title: Arrive Tromsø
days.desc: Arrive into the Arctic capital of Norway, set on an island surrounded by fjords and mountains. Depending on season, settle in beneath the polar night or midnight sun and take a first look at the striking triangular panels of the Arctic Cathedral across the Tromsøysundet strait.
days.experience: On clear winter nights the Aurora Borealis can appear directly over the city — even a short walk along the harbour offers a chance of a sighting.

days.title: Tromsø Island Tour with Arctic Cathedral
days.desc: A guided tour of Tromsøya island taking in the Arctic Cathedral (Ishavskatedralen), the Polar Museum on the old harbour front, and views from the Fjellheisen cable car across the city and surrounding peaks.
days.taste: King crab and reindeer stew are the city's signature dishes — pair either with a Mack beer at Ølhallen, the wood-panelled pub that has served Mack Brewery's beer since 1928, brewed just along the street as the world's northernmost brewery since 1877.

days.title: Snowmobile Safari
days.desc: A full day snowmobile safari of around seven hours through the snow-covered valleys and mountain plateaus surrounding Tromsø, often into the Tamok Valley (Tamokdalen), a broad glacial valley an hour or so inland that's become the region's classic snowmobile terrain.
days.experience: Guides time the return leg for dusk, when clear conditions give a good chance of catching the aurora borealis over the open plateau, away from the city's lights.

days.title: Whale Watching
days.desc: A seven-hour boat tour into the fjords north of Tromsø, typically around Kvaløya and Ringvassøya, in search of orcas and humpback whales, which gather each winter to feed on the herring stocks that migrate into these sheltered waters.

days.title: Reindeer Sledding & Sami Culture
days.desc: Visit a Sami reindeer camp for a longer sledding excursion, reindeer feeding, and an introduction to Sami culture and traditions from an indigenous herding family.
days.taste: Warm up with reindeer stew and bidos cooked over an open fire in a traditional lavvu tent.

days.title: Arctic Fjord Tour
days.desc: A five-hour tour of the fjords around Tromsø with a light lunch included, taking in dramatic coastal scenery and small fishing villages along the way.
days.taste: The included lunch often features fresh cod or bacalao, the salted-cod dish that has sustained Arctic Norway's fishing villages for centuries.

days.title: Free Day in Tromsø
days.desc: A free day to explore Tromsø at your own pace — browse the shops and cafes of the compact city centre, visit the Polaria Arctic experience centre, or simply rest up after a busy week of excursions.
days.taste: Round off the week with cloudberries (multer) and cream, a prized Arctic dessert, or a plate of reindeer at one of the harbourside restaurants below the Arctic Cathedral.
days.shopping: Storgata, Tromsø's main pedestrian street, is lined with Norwegian design, outdoor-gear and souvenir shops.
days.fallbackIncluded: Included: choice of Northern Lights Cruise or Night Reindeer Sledding Tour with Dinner (18h00-22h30).

days.title: Depart Tromsø
days.desc: Free time until your private transfer to Tromsø Airport, depending on flight time.

about.title: Best season
about.body: This is a winter-only programme, best enjoyed November to March for snow activities and the best chance of the Northern Lights.

about.title: Weather
about.body: Arctic winter conditions with temperatures typically -5°C to -15°C, heavy snow cover, and only a few hours of daylight around midwinter.

about.title: Currency & docs
about.body: Norway uses the Norwegian Krone (NOK), though this package is priced in Euros; Norway is part of the Schengen area but not the EU.

goodToKnow.title: Tickets & timing
goodToKnow.body: Snowmobile safaris and whale watching tours should be booked well ahead for the January to March peak season, as availability is limited.

goodToKnow.title: Northern Lights
goodToKnow.body: Sightings depend on clear skies and solar activity and cannot be guaranteed on any single night — the included cruise maximises the chances by heading to clearer coastal areas such as Sommarøy or Ersfjordbotn.

goodToKnow.title: What to pack
goodToKnow.body: Warm thermal layers, insulated waterproof boots and gloves are essential; some tour operators provide thermal suits for snowmobile and boat excursions.

goodToKnow.title: Airport transfers
goodToKnow.body: Private transfers are included between Tromsø Airport and the hotel on arrival and departure days only.

goodToKnow.title: Money
goodToKnow.body: Norway is one of Europe's more expensive destinations; cards are accepted almost everywhere, so carrying cash is rarely necessary — even Ølhallen and the market stalls on Storgata take card payment.

terms: All rates are net and per person for the package.
Child rates apply for children aged 2 to 11 years old. One child sharing a room with 2 adults, subject to hotel policy.
All rates are subject to availability at the time of booking. Europe Incoming will endeavour to match the rates quoted.
Rates are not applicable during trade fair periods, major European public holidays, and other major events.
City taxes are not included in the price.
If certain attractions are closed during specific periods, alternative options will be arranged.
All bookings must be confirmed at least 60 working days prior to arrival.
100% pre-payment required by bank transfer or credit card (Visa or Mastercard). All bank transfer charges covered by the Agent.
Vouchers will be issued after receipt of full payment.
